<think>
Let me analyze the source context to determine if the action boundary is closed.

The task involves checking if a change request (REQ-CHG-903) can be executed at 21:15Z on 2026-07-03.

Let me trace through the sources:
1. REQ-CHG-903: Execute CHG-903 at 21:15Z on 2026-07-03
2. WIN-CHG-903: Approval window from 14:00 to 15:30 PT on 2026-07-03
3. TZ-SITE-Q: Site uses PT, which equals UTC-7 on 2026-07-03
4. APP-CHG-903: Binds CHG-903, site SITE-Q, and approval window WIN-CHG-903
5. POL-CHG-TZ: Change execution requires the execution time to fall inside the approved local site window after timezone conversion

Time conversion:
- PT = UTC-7
- So 21:15Z = 21:15 - 7 = 14:15 PT

The approval window is 14:00 to 15:30 PT.
21:15Z converts to 14:15 PT, which falls WITHIN the 14:00-15:30 PT window.

The execution time of 21:15Z (14:15 PT) is within the approved window (14:00-15:30 PT).

POL-CHG-TZ requires execution time to fall inside the approved local site window after timezone conversion.
- 21:15Z converts to 14:15 PT ✓
- 14:15 PT is within 14:00-15:30 PT ✓

This satisfies all requirements. The action boundary is closed.

All sources are consistent and the dependency is resolved. The turn has no unresolved dependencies from previous turns.
</think>
